overrides.//: Async data-fetch hooks: state is reset/seeded when the fetched resource id changes, not a render-time prop->state mirror. Deliberate reset-on-resource-change effect.
Provider syncs its internal state to incoming config props on resource change (deliberate reset), not a derivable render-time value.
Live-call hook resets streaming/transcript state when the active runId changes (reset-on-resource-change). Not a prop mirror.
Audio recording is fetched in an effect and player state is reset when the recording source changes (legitimate fetch + reset-on-resource-change).
Voice list is fetched in an effect; selection is reset when the fetched options change (legitimate async fetch + reset).
Dependency is react-router's useLocation() result, which is a reactive value intended to be a dependency; the rule's mutable-object heuristic is a false positive here.

no-mutable-in-deps: react-router useLocation() reactive value. no-static-element-interactions (PersonaCard): role/tabIndex/onKeyDown are applied in lockstep with onClick, so the element IS keyboard-accessible at runtime; the analyzer can't prove the conditional role is set.

JudgeRow: role/tabIndex/onKeyDown and onClick are all gated on the same `interactive` flag, so it is keyboard-accessible whenever clickable; the analyzer can't see the conditional role is always paired with onClick.

The `role="button"` clear-filter affordance is rendered INSIDE the Popover trigger <Button>; a native nested <button> is invalid HTML, so role=button on a div is the correct pattern (keyboard handler added).

The clickable row is a `role="button"` wrapper that CONTAINS interactive children (Checkbox, action buttons, menu items); a native <button> cannot nest interactive descendants, so role=button is correct here.
Custom draggable seek bar uses role="slider" (with aria-valuemin/max/now + arrow-key handling); there is no native HTML tag for a styled scrub bar.

role=group on the timestamp-mode segmented control (suggested <address> is semantically wrong) and the ev-row toggle (role=button, fully keyboard-accessible) which is a CSS-grid row that breaks when turned into a native <button>.
role=group on the colour-swatch fieldset wrapper (the swatches themselves are real <button>s); suggested native tag does not fit a labelled radio-like group of colour buttons.

Timeline span blocks + drag-to-seek track are pointer-position visualizations (click maps to an x-coordinate on the trace); there is no meaningful keyboard equivalent and no native tag for a draggable trace surface.
